Yulang `v0.1.0-alpha.2` is an alpha release focused on editor support and
language-server stability.

## Highlights

- Added language-server support for hover, go to definition, find references,
  prepare rename, and rename.
- Improved `yulang server` behavior for files inside `lib/std`; standard
  library files are now analyzed as their `std::...` modules instead of falling
  back to a local-only route.
- Made language-server diagnostics run in the background with timeout guards, so
  slow checks no longer block hover/definition-style requests.
- Fixed inference/effect-stack regressions that could make full-std checks hang
  or overflow.
- Kept release archive and installer smoke coverage for Linux, macOS, and
  Windows.

## Install

Unix-like systems:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://yulang.momota.pw/install.sh | sh -s -- --version v0.1.0-alpha.2
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Invoke-WebRequest https://yulang.momota.pw/install.ps1 -OutFile install.ps1
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\install.ps1 -Version v0.1.0-alpha.2
```

## Known Limitations

- LSP support is still early.
- Record-field references and rename are not covered yet.
- Diagnostics still need clearer type-origin explanations.
- Runtime performance for continuation-heavy programs is still behind the old
  VM, although the current release is usable for examples and editor feedback.
